text:
Admission Date:  2009-08-05              Discharge Date:   1972-02-01

Date of Birth:  1984-10-15             Sex:   M

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Michele
Chief Complaint:
Status post wall falling onto patient. Reportedly was sitting in
the beauty salon waiting for his wife when a car crashed into
the store, collapsing the wall onto the patient and crushing
him.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
12-18: to Interventional Radiology for embolization of bleeding
vessels x 01-23 in the pelvis.
08-24: Left forearm irrigation and debridement of open
wound to left forearm, examination under anesthetic, radius
fracture.
04-21:Tracheostomy, Percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG),
Closed treatment of pelvic ring fracture, with manipulation
under anesthesia


History of Present Illness:
80 year old male status post wall collapse after a car crashed
into beauty salon where he was sitting waiting for his wife.  Jesse Dyer
was intubated at the scene prior to intubation was complaining
of 07-14 chest pressure.

Past Medical History:
Past Medical History: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease,
Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease, hiatal hernia
Past Surgical History: Had a placement of an Metallic AVR, and
CABG

Social History:
Nonsmoker. Retired

Family History:
Noncontributory

Physical Exam:
Physical Exam on Admission
Vital Signs: T 100.4 HR 143 BP 138/99 RR 24 O2Sat 97% on Vent
General: Intubated and Sedated
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm
Lung: Clear to ausculation bilaterally with decreased breath
sounds on the right lower bases, No crepitus
Abdomen: soft, nondistended, nontender
Injuries: Laceration to the chin, to the Right chest with
eccychomosis, Laceration to the Left forearm, bilateral shins
and nose.

Pertinent Results:
CXR on 2009-08-05:
1. Multiple right rib fractures, and small associated right
pneumothorax.
2. Widened mediastinum in this patient with significant chest
trauma raises
concern for vascular injury. Further evaluation with CTA is
recommended.
3. Endotracheal tube nearly reaches the right mainstem bronchus.
Recommend
withdrawing tube 2-3 cm for more optimal positioning
.
CT head on 2009-08-05:No acute intracranial process. No evidence
of fracture. Mucosal thickening in paranasal sinuses.
.
CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis on 2009-08-05:
1. Complex fractures of the pelvis likely unstable with multiple
potential sites of focal extravasation.  Multiple rib fractures.
No definite flail chest. Fracture at left L3 transverse proces,
with focus of likely active extravasation at left erector spine
muscle at L3 level.
2. Resolution effectively of right-sided pneumothorax with
possible traumatic pneumatocele and tip of right-sided chest
tube, which should be withdrawn somewhat. This was discussed at
the time with Dr. Horn. Bilateral consolidation at lung bases,
with bilateral pleural effusion. Mediastinal hematoma.
Subcutanous emphysema as decribed above.
3. Thickenning of obturator and piriformis muscle, more on
right, likely
traumatic hematoma. Blood in perivesical fat.
4. Choledocholythiasis, and gallstones, with mild intrahepatic
bilary duct
dilatation.
.
CT C-spine: No evidence of misalignment or fracture in the
cervical spine.
.
Left Forearm AP/Lateral film: Non-displaced fracture of the
diaphysis of the radius
.
2012-03-17 Echocardiogram: The left atrium is normal in size.
Left ventricular wall thickness, cavity size, and global
systolic function are normal (LVEF>55%). Regional left
ventricular wall motion is normal. The right ventricular cavity
is mildly dilated with normal free wall contractility. The
number of aortic valve leaflets cannot be determined. The aortic
valve is not well seen. No aortic regurgitation is seen. The
mitral valve leaflets are mildly thickened. Trivial mitral
regurgitation is seen. There is mild pulmonary artery systolic
hypertension. There is no pericardial effusion.
.
2001-11-12 CT chest:
1. Enlarging left pleural effusion, now moderate to large, still
layering
along the posterior wall but more heterogeneous, could be due to
blood.
Complete collapse of the left lower lobe. Partial collapse of
the left upper lobe with residual aeration of the anterior
segment.
2. Improving mediastinal hematoma.
3. Persistent very tiny right pneumothorax. Pneumoperitoneum.
Persistent
subcutaneous gas collections.
4. Multiple rib fractures.
5. Unchanged small right pleural effusion and adjacent
atelectasis.
6. Contusions along the old right chest tube tract and slightly
below it with no residual cavitation.
.

Brief Hospital Course:
Mr Haynes was brought in by 77793 Brian Run
Carlaburgh, FM 98043 from the scene at the
hair salon where the patient was sitting inside when a car hit
the hair salon causing the wall to collapse onto the patient.
He was intubated at the scene.  A right chest tube was placed on
the right due a right pneumothorax.  He went to interventional
radiology for bleeding pelvic fracture.  They were able to
emobilize 01-23 vessels.  On HD 2 because of the increasing
hemothorax and the fact that he recieved 15 units of PRBCs, 15
units of FFP, 2 units of platelets and 1 unit of Cryoprecipate
and Amicar drip it was decided to put in a posterior chest tube.
 HD 3 he was in Atrial fibrillation so he was started on a
diltiazem drip.  HD 4 he went to the OR with Ortho for left
upper extremity washout. The left radial fracture was treated
with splint.  He will need to have that left radial fracture
immobilized for a total of 6 weeks.  On HD 5 he was started on a
heparin drip for protection of his mechanical valve.  On HD 6
his anterior chest tube was removed.  On HD 7 he went to the OR
for placement of a tracheostomy and Percutaneous endoscopic
gastrostomy.  Also while he was in the OR his pelvic fracture
was stressed.  He is touch down weight bearing on the left for a
left acetabular fracture.  He is weight bearing as tolerated on
the right lower extremity.  After the OR on HD 7 his posterior
chest tube was pulled.  On HD 9 he spiked a fever and he started
to develop a left pleural effusion.  He was pancultured and he
had a bronch.  Micro showed a E.coli urinary tract infection and
a bronchoalveolar lavage showed Klebsiella and Morganella.  On
HD 10 he was placed on vanco, cipro and zosyn.  On HD 13 a left
chest pigtail was placed which put out 2.5 Liters of bloody
fluid.  On HD 15 his pigtail was removed.  He is on [**Initials (NamePattern4) **] Ramirez-muir
valve.  He is able to eat soft solid with thin liquids.  His
tubefeeds are cycled at night.  He had a calorie count started.
His vanco and zosyn have been discontinued.  He will need a
total course of cipro for 14 days.


Medications on Admission:
Coumadin, simvastatin, ? digoxin, ? dilt

Discharge Medications:
1. Lansoprazole 30 mg Tablet,Rapid Dissolve, DR Cardenas: One (1)
Tablet,Rapid Dissolve, DR Cardenas DAILY (Daily). Tablet,Rapid
Dissolve, DR(s)
2. Clonidine 0.1 mg/24 hr Patch Weekly Cardenas: One (1) Patch Weekly
Transdermal QTUES (every Tuesday).
3. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Cardenas: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed for constipation. Tablet(s)
4. Methadone 10 mg Tablet Cardenas: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day): For Pain for his injuries as pain resolves then we can
slowly wean the methadone.
5. Diltiazem HCl 30 mg Tablet Cardenas: One (1) Tablet PO QID (4
times a day) as needed for afib.
6. Magnesium Hydroxide 400 mg/5 mL Suspension Cardenas: Thirty (30)
ML PO Q6H (every 6 hours) as needed for constipation.
7. Docusate Sodium 50 mg/5 mL Liquid Cardenas: One (1)  PO BID (2
times a day) as needed for constipation.
8. Bisacodyl 5 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Cardenas: Two (2)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily) as needed for
constipation.
9. Hydromorphone 2 mg Tablet Cardenas: 1-2 Tablets PO Q4H (every 4
hours) as needed for pain.
10. Ciprofloxacin 750 mg Tablet Cardenas: One (1) Tablet PO Q12H
(every 12 hours) for 1 weeks.
11. Albuterol Sulfate 90 mcg/Actuation HFA Aerosol Inhaler Cardenas:
Six (6) Puff Inhalation Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed for
wheezing.
12. Warfarin 2.5 mg Tablet Cardenas: One (1) Tablet PO 3X/WEEK
(MO,WE,FR) as needed for Mechanical valve.
13. Warfarin 5 mg Tablet Cardenas: One (1) Tablet PO 4X/WEEK
(Mary,TU,TH,SA).
14. Insulin Regular Human 100 unit/mL Solution Mary: One (1)
units Injection ASDIR (AS DIRECTED).


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Poplar Bluff Regional Medical Center - South - 0319 Angela Mill
Port Laura, WY 39265

Discharge Diagnosis:
Status post wall collapse when he was sitting in the beauty
salon.
His Injuries:
Bilateral rib fractures (~11 total)
Right pneumothorax
Multiple pelvic fractures
Left acetabular fracture
Left comminuted, NOT OPEN radial fracture


Discharge Condition:
Stable

Discharge Instructions:
You were evaluated and cared for in the ICU after sustaining a
major trauma from a crush injury. You are being discharged in
stable condition to a rehabilitation facility. You are being
treated for pneumonia and urinary tract infection.

Please call your surgeon if you develop chest pain, shortness of
breath, fever greater than 101.5, foul smelling or colorful
drainage from your incisions, redness or swelling, severe
abdominal pain or distention, persistent nausea or vomiting,
inability to eat or drink, or any other symptoms which are
concerning to you.

Followup Instructions:
1. Please follow up with plastics hand clinic for the radial
fracture.  Please call 924-897-2251 to make an appointmentin 2
weeks.
2. Please follow up with Orthopediacs for the pelvic fracture
and the left acetabular fracture. Please call 100-427-4830 for
follow up appointment in 01-13 weeks.
3. Please follow up with Plastics Surgery for possible skin
graft placement on the left upper arm and right lower extremity.
 Please call 518-299-3282 for an appointment in 2 weeks.
4. Please follow up with Dr Cardenas in 01-23 weeks for follow up of
your trauma.  Please call (960-309-7490 to make an appointment.
